problem_addressed: Training video diffusion models from scratch is computationally expensive and resource-intensive, limiting accessibility for applications requiring realistic spatiotemporal dynamics.
core_claim: A time-consistency discriminator can guide pretrained image diffusion models to generate realistic time-consistent dynamics without requiring model finetuning or extensions.
key_contributions: Proposed a time-consistency discriminator that guides sampling inference of pretrained image diffusion models
Demonstrated superior uncertainty calibration and lower biases compared to video diffusion models
Achieved stable centennial-scale climate simulations at daily time steps
novelty: Unlike prior work that requires training video diffusion models from scratch or modifying existing models, this approach uses a separate discriminator to guide the sampling process of unmodified pretrained image diffusion models.
method_summary: The method trains a time-consistency discriminator to evaluate temporal coherence between consecutive frames during sampling, then incorporates this discriminator's guidance signal to steer the diffusion process toward temporally consistent outputs without modifying the base image diffusion model.
method_components: Pretrained image diffusion model as the base generator
Time-consistency discriminator trained to evaluate temporal coherence
Guidance mechanism that incorporates discriminator feedback during sampling
key_innovation: The discriminator-guided sampling mechanism that penalizes temporally inconsistent predictions during the diffusion process, enabling time consistency without model finetuning.
assumptions: The pretrained image diffusion model has reasonable spatial generation capabilities
The discriminator can effectively learn temporal consistency patterns from training data
The guidance signal can be incorporated without destabilizing the diffusion process
theoretical_grounding: No formal theoretical proofs or mathematical bounds provided; methodology relies on empirical validation through experiments on turbulence simulations and precipitation data.
title: Generating time-consistent dynamics with discriminator-guided image diffusion models
